problem: How many 3-letter words can we make from the letters A, B, C, and D, if we are allowed to repeat letters, and we must use the letter A at least once? (Here, a word is an arbitrary sequence of letters.)

classified_questions.question: Can you describe the problem using your own words?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: What are the specific conditions for forming a 3-letter word in this problem?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How many total 3-letter words can be made using the letters A, B, C, and D, if we are allowed to repeat letters without any restrictions?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many 3-letter words can be made from just the letters B, C, and D if we are allowed to repeat letters without any restrictions?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many 3-letter words can be made using the letters A, B, C, and D, if we are required to use at least one A and can repeat letters?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How did you determine the total number of words that contain at least one A?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you explain why we subtracted the number of words from just B, C, and D from the total number of 3-letter words?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you think of any other similar problems that involve permutations and combinations?
classified_questions.label: e
